title: GDPR Purpose Limitation Enforcement Across Systems

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).
Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).
NIST 800-53 Rev. 5 PT-3 Personally Identifiable Information Processing Purposes — UC enforces purpose-limitation across systems, providing the evidence that processing stays within declared purposes.

app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services (Splunkbase 3110)
spl:
| tstats `summariesonly` sum(All_Traffic.bytes_out) as bytes_out
  from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic
  where earliest=-7d
  by All_Traffic.src All_Traffic.dest All_Traffic.app
| rename All_Traffic.* as *
| lookup gdpr_integration_purpose_map.csv app OUTPUT ropa_purpose dest_category_allowed
| eval incompatible=if(NOT match(dest_category_allowed, dest_category),1,0)
| where incompatible=1
| table src, dest, app, ropa_purpose, dest_category, dest_category_allowed, bytes_out
| sort - bytes_out
value: Personal data must be collected for specified, explicit, and legitimate purposes and not further processed incompatibly. This use case compares declared processing purposes in ROPA to observed data flows — e.g., HR exports to marketing tools — using purpose tags on integrations where available.
implementation: (1) Document each integration's allowed downstream categories in `gdpr_integration_purpose_map.csv`; (2) populate `dest_category` from proxy and CASB; (3) alert when HR or health-tagged sources send data to marketing-class destinations; (4) require DPO sign-off for exceptions; (5) integrate with access reviews.
visualization: Sankey (source purpose to destination class), Table (incompatible flows), Single value (open violations).
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.app | sort - count

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services

knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
